mission.title: Install and Configure SurrealDB
mission.description: SurrealDB is a scalable, distributed, collaborative, multi-model database designed for the real-time web. It combines the features of document and graph databases, making it suitable for applications that require flexible data models. Key trade-offs include its complexity in setup and management compared to traditional databases.

steps.title: Add the SurrealDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official SurrealDB Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install SurrealDB using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add surrealdb https://charts.surrealdb.com

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the SurrealDB Helm chart repository.
> This updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for SurrealDB to function correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/surrealdb/main/deploy/crds/surrealdbs.surrealdb.com_customresourcedefinition.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs SurrealDB in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --version 3.0.2 --namespace surrealdb --create-namespace

```

> This installs SurrealDB in the 'surrealdb' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for SurrealDB to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: example-surrealdb
  namespace: surrealdb
spec:
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This creates a simple SurrealDB instance.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: example-surrealdb
  namespace: surrealdb
spec:
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the SurrealDB instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the SurrealDB instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get surrealdb -n surrealdb

```

> This command lists the SurrealDB instances in the 'surrealdb' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall SurrealDB
uninstall.description:
This step removes SurrealDB and its associated resources from your cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless you have backups.

```bash

helm uninstall surrealdb --namespace surrealdb

kubectl delete crd surrealdbs.surrealdb.com

```

> This command uninstalls the SurrealDB release.
> This command removes the Custom Resource Definitions.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SurrealDB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This process will upgrade SurrealDB to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get surrealdb -n surrealdb -o json > surrealdb-backup.json

helm upgrade surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --version 3.0.2 --namespace surrealdb

kubectl get surrealdb -n surrealdb

```

> Backup the current SurrealDB instance.
> This command upgrades SurrealDB to the specified version.
> Verify that the SurrealDB instance is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get surrealdb -n surrealdb -o json > surrealdb-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'SurrealDB instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified SurrealDB instance does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the namespace and ensure the SurrealDB instance is created correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to SurrealDB'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB service is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the SurrealDB pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n surrealdb' and check logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n surrealdb'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions for SurrealDB have not been installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using the provided kubectl command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the SurrealDB pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the minimum resource requirements (2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node) and scale up if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for multi-model queries and enhanced performance for concurrent connections.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the SurrealDB instance listed with 'kubectl get surrealdb -n surrealdb' and its status should be 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: example-surrealdb
  namespace: surrealdb
spec:
  replicas: 1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
